indianaJonesWalkthrough.title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle Complete Walkthrough
indianaJonesWalkthrough.intro: Follow the main adventure order from South America to Iraq, with the major Fieldwork and Mystery checks placed where they fit best. Use this route for a clean story run or a 100% cleanup path.

steps.title: South America: The Golden Idol
steps.description: Finish the opening temple sequence, learn basic traversal, traps, item handling, and the idol escape. This is a focused tutorial chapter.

steps.title: Marshall College: The Break-In
steps.description: Investigate the museum break-in, inspect the college rooms, and follow the clues that lead Indy toward the Vatican. Collect early Journal Notes around classrooms and Indy's office.

steps.title: The Vatican: The Stolen Cat Mummy
steps.description: First large hub route: meet Antonio, get the camera from Ernesto, explore the Vatican, solve story puzzles, and push toward the underground path. Clear nearby Fieldwork and Mysteries while you have access.

steps.title: Gizeh: The Race Is On / The Idol of Ra
steps.description: Arrive in Gizeh, learn the desert hub layout, locate key camps and dig sites. Then progress through pyramid, Sphinx, tablet, and light-based puzzle sections. Prioritize Cloud Atlas for the Gizeh Medicine book.

steps.title: The Himalayas: A Harsh Climb
steps.description: Move through the KMS Kummetz section, solve the Kummetz Cipher door, and escape the frozen ship route. Check side rooms for Journal Notes before advancing.

steps.title: Shanghai: Into the Fire
steps.description: Complete the high-pressure Shanghai escape sequence and collect available notes along the path.

steps.title: Sukhothai: Trouble in Paradise / The Blessed Pearl
steps.description: Enter the Sukhothai hub, unlock boat movement, learn the water routes. Then complete Wat Mahathat, the hidden pyramid, and late-game temple puzzles. Clear all safe codes and cogwheel puzzles before the final push.

steps.title: Iraq: Atonements
steps.description: Finish the final story chapter and use the endgame route for the Secret Vault and final completion checks. Return through free-roam to finish remaining collectibles.

quickTips: Progress each hub naturally and clear nearby Fieldwork and Mysteries while you have access.
Buy Vatican, Gizeh, and Sukhothai exploration books when available to mark collectibles on the map.
Use post-story free-roam and the Journal travel tab for final cleanup.
Keep regional money separate because each region uses its own currency.

indianaJonesSafeCodes.title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle Safe Codes
indianaJonesSafeCodes.intro: Use these combinations for locked safes, chests, lockboxes, and story doors across the Vatican, Gizeh, the Himalayas, and Sukhothai. Filter by region, copy the code, then check the location and requirement note.

cards.name: Belvedere Courtyard Chest (Chest)
cards.description: Code: 5238. Location: Tent in the Belvedere Courtyard. Reward: Splinter Smash. Find the nearby ticket-stub clue.

cards.name: Secret of Secrets (Safe)
cards.description: Code: 4471. Location: Office west of the Sistine Chapel. Reward: Cutman I. Decode the saint-statue clues.

cards.name: A Date to Remember (Chest)
cards.description: Code: 6380. Location: Restricted area between Belvedere Courtyard and Museum Wing. Reward: Vatican Medicine.

cards.name: Post Office Cellar Lockbox
cards.description: Code: 1136. Location: Cellar beneath the post office on Via Di Belvedere. Reward: Street Scrapper I. Get the Cellar Key during A Savage Discovery.

cards.name: Cloud Atlas (Chest)
cards.description: Code: 0609. Location: Meteorological Station northwest of Gizeh Workers' Area. Reward: Gizeh Medicine. Match cloud status dates to atlas page numbers.

cards.name: Nazi Compound Safe
cards.description: Code: 40926. Location: Building with Voss' office inside the Nazi Compound. Reward: Gun, money, and Ancient Relic.

cards.name: Kummetz Cipher (Door)
cards.description: Code: 3666. Location: Officer's Quarters on the KMS Kummetz. Reward: Story progression. Set code wheels, enter Captain's Code JÖGM, read BLUT.

cards.name: Voss' Gold Stash Safe
cards.description: Code: 5484. Location: Deep inside Voss' camp in Sukhothai. Reward: Voss' gold stash. Knock out the guard near the safe for the combination.

indianaJonesPuzzleSolutions.title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle Puzzle Solutions
indianaJonesPuzzleSolutions.intro: Quick answers for the most common blockers, including Vatican fountain puzzles, Gizeh chamber puzzles, code-machine puzzles, and Sukhothai board or gear puzzles. Each entry gives the puzzle location, shortest solution, and reward.

items.name: Fountain of Confession
items.description: Turn both dragon statues to face the fountain, whip the center dragon, rotate the fountain cube. Opens the underground route toward the Secret Monastery.

items.name: Sacred Wounds
items.description: Pour wine into golden basins, read residue numbers beside marked wounds, match numbers to levers on crucifix mural. Opens next Vatican story path.

items.name: House of God
items.description: Slide front lever on puzzle box, move medallion into opposite compartment, take Golden Chalice, place on top. Reward: Vatican Relics book.

items.name: Chamber of Resonance
items.description: Photograph Adamic tablets, crawl into low tunnel, break wall with two-handed mace, select photos in order: Heed, The Lord's, Oath, Protect, The secret, Of the name. Opens Underground Cave System.

items.name: Cloud Atlas
items.description: Match dates 13.10, 09.10, 16.10, 14.10 with Cirrus, Stratus, Cirrus, Cumulonimbus, convert to 0, 6, 0, 9. Reward: Gizeh Medicine book.

items.name: Kummetz Cipher
items.description: Collect four code wheels, arrange in cipher machine, enter Captain's Code JÖGM, read BLUT, cross-check on code tables, input 3666. Unlocks KMS Kummetz story door.

items.name: A Game of Wits
items.description: Solve the Mak-Yek board puzzle, then use combination 3186 to open the safe. Reward: Mystery completion.

items.name: Khmer Cogwheel Puzzles
items.description: Collect all 10 Khmer Cogwheels, connect each gear train, recover Ancient Relic at each site. Reward: Ancient Relics and Gear Head completion route.

indianaJonesCollectibles.title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle Collectibles
indianaJonesCollectibles.intro: Track the major collectible groups needed for 100% completion, trophies, upgrades, and late-game cleanup. Start with region maps and exploration books, then clean up remaining items through free-roam.

solutions.name: Ancient Relics
solutions.description: The Vatican 10, Gizeh 15, Sukhothai 24, Iraq 1. Required for Shadows out of Time and Secret Vault completion.

solutions.name: Lost Artifacts
solutions.description: The Vatican 5, Gizeh 5, Sukhothai 5. Required for Repatriation.

solutions.name: Journal Notes
solutions.description: All major regions. Required for Archivist. Split across Adventure, Fieldwork, Discoveries, and Mysteries.

solutions.name: Adventure Books
solutions.description: Major hubs, vendors, locked chests, puzzle rewards. Required for Bookman. Used to unlock or improve Indy abilities with Adventure Points.

solutions.name: Stelae
solutions.description: Gizeh only. Required for It Belongs in a Museum. Collect while clearing Gizeh dig sites.

solutions.name: Radio Frequencies
solutions.description: Gizeh 4, Sukhothai 4. Required for Tuned In. Collect from Nazi or Fascist compounds.

solutions.name: Khmer Cogwheels
solutions.description: Sukhothai only. Used for cogwheel puzzles and the final Khmer Gear Puzzle route.

solutions.name: Medicine Bottles
solutions.description: The Vatican, Gizeh, Sukhothai. Used with doctors to obtain upgrade books for health and stamina progression.

managementTips: Buy regional exploration books first so collectible icons appear on the map.
Sweep each hub after finishing the main route in that region.
Use the Journal to check photos, letters, maps, and clue documents after each major quest.
Collect Ancient Relics through hub puzzles and underground routes, not just as loot.

indianaJonesAdventureBooks.title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle Adventure Books
indianaJonesAdventureBooks.intro: Adventure Books are the core long-term upgrade system in Indiana Jones and the Great Circle. Track high-value unlocks early, then use map reveal books to speed up collectible cleanup in each major region.

cards.name: Punch Out I
cards.description: Region: The Vatican, Castel Sant'Angelo. Cost: 25 AP. Effect: restores stamina after a knockout. Priority: unlock early.

cards.name: Lucky Hat I
cards.description: Region: The Vatican, Captain's Room. Cost: 125 AP. Effect: second-chance recovery after getting knocked out.

cards.name: Moxie I, II, III
cards.description: Regions: Vatican, Gizeh, Sukhothai. Cost: 5/10/15 Medicine Bottles. Effect: extends stamina bar for exploration and combat.

cards.name: Shaping Up I, II, III
cards.description: Regions: Vatican, Gizeh, Sukhothai. Cost: 5/10/15 Medicine Bottles. Effect: adds extra health reserve bars.

highlights: Climbing Ace I and Pep I are strong early stamina picks for route efficiency.
Throttle and Jumbo Lasso improve stealth and heavy-enemy control in tougher encounters.
Fruit Bag is a practical sustain option when staying in the field longer.
Use Vatican, Gizeh, and Sukhothai map books before 100% cleanup.

indianaJonesTrophies.title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle Trophies and Achievements
indianaJonesTrophies.intro: Use this roadmap to plan a one-playthrough base-game completion route, then finish The Order of Giants DLC cleanup for full platform completion totals.

regions.name: Base PlayStation Trophies
regions.description: Includes 1 Platinum, 2 Gold, 15 Silver, and 28 Bronze. Typical completion time: 40-50 hours.

regions.name: Base Xbox Achievements
regions.description: Follows the same route as Steam completion: story progress, Fieldwork, Mysteries, collectibles, and Adventure Books.

regions.name: Missables and Difficulty
regions.description: Difficulty does not gate the listed trophies, and free-roam cleanup remains available after story completion.

regions.name: Hardest Completion Check
regions.description: Requires all Journal notes and effectively represents full base-game completion discipline.

indianaJonesDLC.title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle The Order of Giants DLC
indianaJonesDLC.intro: The Order of Giants is a Rome-focused story DLC. Follow this sequence to unlock it correctly, clear the chapter efficiently, and complete the DLC collectible set.

creatures.name: Step 1: Check DLC Access
creatures.description: Released September 4, 2025. Included with Premium Edition/Upgrade and Collector bundles; base game required.

creatures.name: Step 2: Reach Required Story Point
creatures.description: Advance main story until Vatican progress allows DLC start, including the related progression objective checkpoint.

creatures.name: Step 3: Start A Mystery Begins
creatures.description: Meet Father Ricci near the Vatican Fountain of Confession to begin the DLC route.

creatures.name: Step 4: Enter Villa Pia Route
creatures.description: Follow Pio, access Ricci's office, collect key items, and push into the Casina/Villa Pia objective chain.

creatures.name: Step 5: Clear Rome Chapter
creatures.description: Complete the connected Rome story thread across underground routes, chambers, and puzzle-heavy segments.

creatures.name: Step 6: Collect DLC Completion Set
creatures.description: Collect 118 Journal Notes, 7 Mithraic Artifacts, and complete all Rome Mysteries plus book abilities.

creatures.name: Step 7: Finish Trophy/Achievement Targets
creatures.description: Wrap story completion, remaining mysteries, artifact checks, and the optional cat interaction objective.

indianaJonesPlatforms.title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle Platforms and PC Requirements
indianaJonesPlatforms.intro: Before buying or installing, check platform availability, edition benefits, Game Pass coverage, and PC hardware tiers including ray tracing requirements.

items.name: Xbox Series X|S
items.description: Available with Xbox ecosystem support and Play Anywhere benefits when purchased through Xbox.

items.name: Steam and PlayStation 5
items.description: Steam supports PC requirements and achievements; PS5 supports the full trophy route including Platinum.

items.name: Nintendo Switch 2
items.description: Listed as available with release date May 12, 2026 for portable play coverage.

items.name: Game Pass Access
items.description: Available through Game Pass options for Xbox Series X|S, Xbox PC, and cloud-supported play paths.

items.name: Standard vs Premium
items.description: Standard includes the base game; Premium adds The Order of Giants DLC and bundled digital extras.

items.name: PC Requirements
items.description: Minimum 1080p tier starts at RTX 2060 SUPER / RX 6600 class; higher tiers target 1440p and 4K with stronger ray tracing GPUs.

unlockMilestones: Check hardware ray tracing support before install.
Confirm SSD capacity: 126 GB plus additional DLC space.
Match edition choice to DLC and bonus content goals.
Use platform-specific ecosystem features to reduce total cost.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Indiana Jones and the Great Circle

questions.question: Is Indiana Jones and the Great Circle a singleplayer game?
questions.answer: Yes. Indiana Jones and the Great Circle is a singleplayer-only game with no multiplayer or co-op planned.

questions.question: What is the Adventure Progression system in Indiana Jones and the Great Circle?
questions.answer: Adventure Progression is Indiana Jones and the Great Circle' freeform fusion crafting system. Instead of following fixed recipes, you combine materials and discover your own crafting paths.

questions.question: Where can I play Indiana Jones and the Great Circle?
questions.answer: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle is currently available on Steam for Windows. Linux and Steam Deck support is available through Proton 9.0.

questions.question: How much does Indiana Jones and the Great Circle cost?
questions.answer: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle costs $9.99 on Steam.

questions.question: Does Indiana Jones and the Great Circle support controllers?
questions.answer: Yes, but controller play is not recommended because the menus are not designed around it. Keyboard and mouse is the smoother option.

questions.question: Will Indiana Jones and the Great Circle receive updates?
questions.answer: Yes. Some content updates are planned after release, but they are not intended to continue on an indefinite regular schedule.
